subject.name: Maths
subject.description: Functional maths in a trade context — areas, volumes, ratios and percentages.

quizzes.title: Trade Maths Workout
quizzes.description: The sums you actually do on site.

questions.text: A cylinder is 1.5 m tall with an internal diameter of 450 mm. What is its capacity in litres? (Nearest litre.)
questions.explain: V = πr²h = π × 0.225² × 1.5 = 0.2386 m³. 1 m³ = 1000 litres → 239 litres.

questions.text: Mortar is mixed 1:5 cement to sand. How many kg of cement are in 300 kg of mix?
questions.explain: 1:5 means 6 parts total. 300 ÷ 6 = 50 kg cement, 250 kg sand.

questions.text: A job is quoted at £840 plus VAT at 20%. What is the total?

questions.explain: × 1000. This conversion catches people out constantly on cylinder sizing.

questions.text: A pipe run rises 1 in 40 over 12 m. What is the rise in millimetres?
A triangular gable is 6 m at the base and 2.4 m high. What is its area in m²?
